docs.title: Asyncio
docs.text:
async func could be await-ed in jupyter lab directly:

import asyncio
import websockets
import datetime

async def attach(uri, n=5):
    data = []
    async with websockets.connect(uri) as ws:
        for i in range(n):
            recv = await ws.recv()
            print(recv)
            data.append(recv)
    return data

data = await attach(endpoint)
len(data)

docs.title: Debian
docs.text:
Debian services

  • systemctl
  • journalctl

systemctl manages services

sudo systemctl reboot
sudo systemctl shutdown

view log ssh

journalctl -t sshd

k8s:

minikube, kind are cluster managers.
